description: Tenant-10 revenue-cycle-management package (ninth test case, reference tenant). Arrived already in risk-engine language and maps onto existing mechanism: the Risk Register is riskType-tagged scores (Batch 2 R11) whose multiplicative index uses the kernel CalculateMultiplicativeRiskScore-equivalent formula (Batch 3 R16) written inline as a KPI formula; the denials->AR->churn chain is EntityRelationship edges (R13); Business Health / Revenue Cycle rollups are CompositeHealth (R14); churn/burnout predictions are Classification forecasts (R15); OIG monthly exclusion screening is a recurring ComplianceRenewal (R17); HIPAA/OIG places the tenant at the Regulated sensitivity tier (R6). Boundary vs ecarmf.ai-financial-analyst: underpayment/overpayment financial analysis is delegated to the shared Financial Analyst agent, not re-implemented here.
name: AI Tenant-10 RCM

entities.description: One PHI/HIPAA risk-register entry, scored by the multiplicative risk index. Factors are 1-5 severity scales except aiConfidence (0-1).
Claim-denial risk-register entry per payer, same 7-factor model.
Credentialing/revalidation-delay risk-register entry per provider, same 7-factor model.
Cybersecurity/ransomware risk-register entry per system, same 7-factor model.
A department/client revenue-cycle period snapshot: the inputs to the denials->AR->cash->churn chain and the Revenue Cycle composite.
One coder's period productivity/accuracy - the input to compensation-relevant per-USER KPIs (Batch 2 R7).
One biller's period productivity/accuracy - per-USER KPIs.

events.description: A record has been durably persisted and is ready for KPI evaluation.

capabilities.name: Revenue-Cycle Intelligence
capabilities.description: Risk register, revenue-cycle health, and workforce KPIs for the Tenant-10 medical-billing tenant.

schemaTemplates.name: Revenue-cycle period report (JSON) - clearinghouse/835-ERA roll-up, reference until vendor details arrive

performanceFrameworks.name: Tenant-10 Risk Register v1
performanceFrameworks.description: The Risk Register as riskType-tagged KPIs (R11). Each KPI's formula is the multiplicative risk index (R16): Likelihood x Business x Compliance x Financial x Reputation x Recovery x AI-Confidence, written inline over the assessment's factor fields; higher index = higher risk (direction lower). Distinct trigger record types keep each category's riskType tag exact.

kpis.name: PHI breach risk index
kpis.formula: likelihood * businessImpact * complianceImpact * financialImpact * reputationImpact * recoveryTime * aiConfidence

kpis.name: Claim denial risk index
kpis.formula: likelihood * businessImpact * complianceImpact * financialImpact * reputationImpact * recoveryTime * aiConfidence

kpis.name: Credentialing delay risk index
kpis.formula: likelihood * businessImpact * complianceImpact * financialImpact * reputationImpact * recoveryTime * aiConfidence

kpis.name: Cybersecurity risk index
kpis.formula: likelihood * businessImpact * complianceImpact * financialImpact * reputationImpact * recoveryTime * aiConfidence

performanceFrameworks.name: Tenant-10 Revenue Cycle KPI Framework v1
performanceFrameworks.description: Departmental revenue-cycle health: coder accuracy, denial rate (riskType ClaimDenial), clean-claim rate, AR aging, cash collection. These are the child scores a Revenue Cycle CompositeHealth rolls up (R14).

performanceFrameworks.name: Tenant-10 Workforce KPI Framework v1
performanceFrameworks.description: Per-USER coder/biller accuracy and productivity (Batch 2 R7: subjectType User). Targets are baselines pending leadership review; once adjusted, the human number is authoritative. Boundary vs tenant10-revenue-cycle-v1: those are department-level, these are person-level.

steps.message: A Tenant-10 metric breached its threshold - review the risk register and revenue-cycle board.

knowledgeAssets.title: HIPAA Security Rule - safeguards summary
knowledgeAssets.summary: Administrative, physical, and technical safeguards for ePHI; risk analysis and risk management are required, not optional.

knowledgeAssets.title: HIPAA Breach Notification Rule - obligations summary
knowledgeAssets.summary: Notification timelines and thresholds after a breach of unsecured PHI; the 60-day outer limit and the 500-record HHS/media threshold.

knowledgeAssets.title: OIG exclusion screening SOP (monthly)
knowledgeAssets.summary: Screen every employee, contractor, and vendor against the OIG LEIE monthly. This is a recurring re-verification (ComplianceRenewal recurrenceMonths=1), never a one-time expiry.

agents.name: Compliance & HIPAA Guide
agents.description: Grounded HIPAA/OIG guidance over the knowledge graph and the risk register. Boundary vs cybersecurity-guide: this agent covers regulatory obligations; technical posture is the cybersecurity guide's.
agents.persona: You are the compliance and HIPAA guide for a medical-billing / revenue-cycle-management company. You answer only from the HIPAA and OIG knowledge assets provided and the compliance-tagged risk scores and deviations you can see, citing the specific asset and its effective date. You explain obligations and flag risk; you never assert that a breach has legally occurred or that an obligation has been met - a qualified compliance officer decides that.
sampleQuestions: Which PHI-breach risks are trending worst right now?
What does the Breach Notification Rule require if a laptop with ePHI is lost?
Are we current on OIG exclusion screening?
prohibited: never state that a reportable breach has legally occurred
never confirm an OIG/HIPAA obligation is satisfied - only that evidence is present or missing
agents.outputDisclaimer: Guidance only - a licensed compliance officer, not this agent, makes HIPAA/OIG determinations.

agents.name: Revenue Cycle Guide
agents.description: Watches the denials -> AR -> cash -> churn chain and the Revenue Cycle composite. Boundary vs ecarmf.ai-financial-analyst: underpayment/overpayment statement analysis is delegated to the shared Financial Analyst.
agents.persona: You are the revenue-cycle advisor for a medical-billing company. You read departmental revenue-cycle KPIs (coder accuracy, denial rate, clean-claim rate, AR days, cash collected), risk-tagged deviations, and the CompositeHealth rollups. You explain where the revenue cycle is leaking and which upstream driver (coder accuracy, a specific payer's denials) is most responsible, and you recommend - never claim an action was taken.
sampleQuestions: Why is Revenue Cycle health down this period?
Is AR aging getting worse in any department?
agents.outputDisclaimer: Guidance only - operational and financial decisions are made by Tenant-10 leadership.

agents.name: Executive Risk Guide
agents.description: Orchestrates the risk register, correlation chain, composite health, and predictions into an executive view. Boundary vs revenue-cycle-guide: this agent is enterprise-wide risk synthesis, not revenue-cycle operations.
agents.persona: You are the executive risk advisor for a medical-billing company. You synthesize the risk register (all riskType-tagged scores), the CompositeHealth rollups, and the classification predictions into a board-level picture, prioritizing by index and trajectory. You present risk and recommendations; you never decide or claim to have acted.
sampleQuestions: What are our top five enterprise risks this quarter?
Which clients are most likely to churn?
agents.outputDisclaimer: Guidance only - the board and executive team make the decisions; this agent synthesizes risk signals.
